note: Temporary, issue-owned direct-access allowances introduced by mainline drift. Every entry has an exact per-kind ceiling and must decrease as #249/#251/#252/#253 are completed.

lib/blockPatchIncrementalIndexes.ts.reason: Main introduced incremental Block Patch index maintenance after the reviewed baseline. It executes inside the existing core note/block SQLite transaction and must migrate with the complete #249 block transaction boundary.

lib/noteBlocks.ts.reason: Main added block-level note operations after the original Repository boundary snapshot. These business operations must move behind the runtime adapter in a later #249 core-note batch.

lib/noteLinkTitles.ts.reason: Main added note-link title hydration after the reviewed baseline; the note-link runtime Repository batch owns this boundary.

lib/searchIndex.ts.reason: Search index maintenance is part of the deferred PostgreSQL FTS/vector redesign and must not be partially rewritten during branch synchronization.

middleware/api-token-resource-scope.ts.reason: Main introduced API token resource enforcement with synchronous SQLite lookups. Token resource writes are migrated in 0005; request-time scope enforcement remains a dedicated #249 async boundary task.

routes/blocks.ts.reason: The new block API arrived after the baseline and requires a block Repository before PostgreSQL business routes can be enabled.

routes/notebooks.ts.reason: Main added share-limit and capability operations to the notebook route. The underlying share Repository is migrated, while remaining route-local notebook transactions await the core notebook batch.

routes/notes.ts.reason: Main expanded note operations after the original snapshot. Core note queries and transactions remain an explicit high-risk #249 batch.

routes/remote-image-import.ts.reason: Remote image import was added on main and still performs note/attachment lookups directly; attachment Repository migration owns it.

routes/search.ts.reason: Main expanded SQLite FTS fallback queries. Search and vector migration are explicitly deferred to #252.

routes/system-update.ts.reason: System update state now reads the database directly and needs a small runtime settings Repository boundary.

routes/wechat-favorites-import.ts.reason: WeChat favorites import is a data migration/import path and is intentionally owned by the SQLite-to-PostgreSQL migration tooling stage.

runtime/embedding-queue-hardening.ts.reason: Main added SQLite-only startup and lease recovery for embedding queues. The hardened entrypoint loads it only after selecting SQLite; PostgreSQL mode never evaluates this module. Cross-driver queue recovery belongs to the #252 search/vector redesign.

services/attachment-reference.ts.reason: The synchronous path remains inside noteTransfer's SQLite transaction. A PostgreSQL Runtime async path now exists, but the legacy exception cannot be removed until the whole transfer transaction is migrated.

services/embedding-worker.ts.reason: The embedding worker remains part of the deferred SQLite vector/search boundary. Its PostgreSQL redesign is owned by #252.

services/noteTransfer.ts.reason: Note transfer is a high-risk multi-table transaction added after the baseline and requires an explicit cross-driver transaction design.

services/nowenPackageExport.ts.reason: Main expanded Nowen package export with an additional database read. Export consistency and cross-driver streaming belong to the #251 migration tooling stage.

services/nowenPackageImport.ts.reason: Main expanded Nowen package import metadata handling. The bulk copy/checkpoint PostgreSQL implementation remains owned by #251.

services/nowenPackageImportV2.ts.reason: The V2 round-trip importer performs a single atomic multi-table copy transaction. It must move as a whole to the cross-driver import transaction layer rather than being partially converted.

services/nowenRoundTripSync.ts.reason: Round-trip incremental sync combines source mapping, notebook/note/attachment writes and object storage cleanup in one transaction boundary. Repository extraction is tracked by the next #251 sync batch.

services/roundTripImportBatches.ts.reason: Batch metadata now has a Runtime Repository and migrations 0009/0010. The remaining direct accesses implement snapshot capture and destructive undo across notebooks, notes, tags, attachments and object storage; they are kept intact for the next atomic #251 transaction batch.

services/roundTripImportLinkUndo.ts.reason: Source-link undo refines and restores mappings around the import undo transaction. It remains coupled to the destructive rollback path and is deferred as one #251 cross-driver transaction unit.

services/roundTripMarkdownExportJobs.ts.reason: Round-trip Markdown export jobs were added after the baseline and are part of the import/export data migration surface tracked by #251.

services/single-share-access.ts.reason: Main consolidated single-share access into a service that still owns SQLite transactions. The share/ACL runtime batch must migrate it as one atomic unit.

services/siyuanPackageImportLegacy.ts.reason: The legacy SiYuan importer gained additional SQLite operations on main; import migration remains explicitly deferred to #251.

services/wechatFavoritesPackageImport.ts.reason: The WeChat package importer is a bulk data migration path and must be redesigned with the #251 copy/checkpoint/resume tooling.
